articles.full_text:
Heatwave Alert: Odisha Govt Limits Outdoor Work Between 11 AM and 3 PM
Anticipating rising temperatures and increased risk of heatwave-related illnesses, the Odisha government has issued precautionary guidelines to protect workers during the summer months. In an advisory to officials including Revenue Divisional Commissioners, District Collectors, and the State Labour Commissioner, the Labour and Employees’ State Insurance Department directed authorities to implement measures to prevent heatstroke. The guidelines will be in effect from April 1 to June 15, 2026.
As per the directive, employers across government and private sectors—including industries, commercial establishments, and contractors—have been instructed to avoid engaging workers in outdoor activities between 11 AM and 3 PM, when heat intensity is highest. They have also been advised to modify working hours accordingly.
For essential services or emergency duties where work during these hours is unavoidable, employers must ensure proper safety measures such as drinking water, shaded rest areas, medical support, and ORS (Oral Rehydration Solution) for workers.
Meanwhile, healthcare systems across the state have been placed on alert. Officials, including CDMOs, SDMOs, and staff at PHCs, CHCs, and ESI hospitals, have been directed to remain prepared to manage heat-related cases effectively.

articles.title: Odisha heatwave: State govt bars outdoor labour work from 11 am to 3 pm - The New Indian Express
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
BHUBANESWAR : With summer expected to turn harsher from next month onwards, the state government on Monday announced restriction in outdoor work for workers and labourers for four hours from 11 am to 3 pm, with effect from April 1.
Issuing a directive to this effect, the Labour and ESI department said the restriction will remain in force till June 15 to protect workers from the risks of heatstroke and intense heat.
The department asked all revenue divisional commissioners, district collectors and the state labour commissioner to implement the measures to reduce heat exposure for labourers during the peak summer period. It further ordered the government departments, central government offices, private employers, industrial and commercial establishments, shop owners, business proprietors and contractors to strictly adhere to the guideline.
As per the department guideline, outdoor strenuous work will be restricted for labourers across all sectors between 11 am and 3 pm from April 1 to June 15 to protect them from heat-related problems, especially during heatwave conditions.
Accordingly, it advised employers to reschedule their work timings, wherever necessary, to ensure labourers do not work outside during the peak heat hours. They have also been directed to provide safe drinking water, sheds and rest areas, on-site medical support and ORS packets etc., to help workers and labourers stay hydrated and recover quickly from heat-related stress.
Medical officers at district-level healthcare facilities, SDMOs, PHCs, CHCs and hospitals under the ESI scheme have been asked to remain prepared.

articles.full_text:
Bureau,Odishabart
Bhubaneswar,( 23/03/26): With temperatures set to climb sharply in April, the Odisha government on Monday rolled out a series of heat wave safety measures to protect workers across the state during the peak summer period.
In an advisory issued by the Labour and Employees’ State Insurance Department, authorities have been asked to take proactive steps to prevent heatstroke and related illnesses.
The guidelines, applicable from April 1 to June 15, 2026, have been sent to Revenue Divisional Commissioners, District Collectors and the State Labour Commissioner.
Employers across government offices, central establishments, private firms, industries, commercial units and contractors have been directed to avoid engaging workers in outdoor work between 11 AM and 3 PM, when heat exposure is at its highest. They have also been advised to reschedule working hours accordingly.
For essential services and emergency duties where work during these hours cannot be avoided, strict safeguards have been mandated. These include access to safe drinking water, shaded rest spaces, medical assistance and provision of ORS packets
The state has also put its healthcare system on alert. Chief District Medical Officers, Sub-Divisional Medical Officers and staff at PHCs, CHCs and ESI hospitals have been instructed to stay prepared to manage heat-related cases.
# agencies

articles.title: No work during day’s peak hours from April 1, Odisha govt issues notification - The Hindu
articles.full_text:
The Odisha government on Monday (March 23, 2026) restricted working hours for all government, non-government, industrial establishments and construction sectors from April 1 in view of rising mercury level in the ensuing summer season.
Stating that in view of the rising temperatures expected in April this year, the possibility of heatstroke during the summer season cannot be ruled out, the government has changed working hours to protect workers engaged in outdoor labour from extreme heat conditions.
The Department of Labour and Employees’ State Insurance has issued a notification intimating instructions to all revenue commissioners, district collectors, and the State labour commissioner in this regard.
“All departments of the Odisha government, as well as all departments of the central government institutes located in Odisha, private employers, all commercial and industrial establishments, shop owners, business establishments, and contractors are requested to ensure that workers under their supervision are not engaged in work between 11 AM and 3 PM from April 1 to June 15,” says the notification.
The department allowed institutes to make necessary adjustments in working hours to safeguard them from severe heat conditions.
“If any work is considered urgent or is related to essential services where workers cannot be given rest during the above-mentioned hours, employers must take precautionary measures to protect them from extreme heat,” the labour department further said.
“These measures must mandatorily include provision of safe drinking water, adequate shaded resting areas, medical facilities including access to doctors, and ORS packets for every worker,” it said. Health department institutions have been requested to remain prepared to handle heat-related conditions and provide necessary treatment to affected patients.
Meteorological Centre, Bhubaneswar on Monday predicted gradual rise in maximum temperature (day temperature) by 2 to 4 degree Celsius during next four to five days. This month, maximum daytime temperature has already breached 40 degree Celsius in some places.
Published - March 24, 2026 03:55 am IST
